primary_method.name: SOTP
primary_method.reasoning: TSMC operates as two economically distinct segments — an AI/HPC leading-edge engine (hyper-growth, >90% market share, pricing power, high reinvestment) and a mature smartphone/IoT/auto annuity (slow growth, stable returns). These have different growth rates, economics, risk profiles, and appropriate multiples. Blending them into a single curve or multiple obscures the real value driver (the size of the AI pool) and manufactures a wrong multiple for both. CONTRACT §2.1 and METHODS.md mandate segment-first SOTP with each segment's own WACC. The most efficient method per segment: HPC/leading-edge is high-growth -> exit-multiple; mature is an annuity -> current earnings x justified P/E.

segments.name: HPC_LeadingEdge
segments.reasoning: AI/HPC platform = 61% of 2025 revenue and growing toward 70% by 2029. At 54-56% AI-accelerator CAGR (management guide, Q1 2026 call), this is genuinely hyper-growth. A forced DCF would require g>r in early years and muddle ROIC; the exit-multiple method normalizes to 2029 (when growth is still ~mid-teens but decelerating) and discounts back at the segment WACC. The normalization year is 2029 -- end of the management guidance window, N2/A16 at scale, CoWoS at 120-130k wpm. Method per CONTRACT §2.2.

exit_inputs.reasoning: NOPAT_T = company 2029 revenue $279.7B (25% CAGR from 2024 $91.7B, per management guide) x 70% HPC mix (growing from 61% in 2025 as AI accelerates) x 60% EBIT margin (above blended 51% in 2025; Q2 2026 guided 56.5-58.5% blended OM with HPC-only higher; leading-edge at utilization achieves >65% OM) x 83% (1 minus 17% effective tax, Taiwan statutory 20% less R&D credits). Gordon exit multiple: (1 - g_lt/ROIC)/(r - g_lt) = (1 - 0.08/0.30)/(0.105 - 0.08) = 0.733/0.025 = 29.33x. Use 27x (modestly below Gordon; acknowledges g_lt could prove slightly lower than 8% if AI-HPC growth decelerates faster post-2029; cross-check: a foundry with monopoly-moat at 8% growth deserves 25-30x by mature-tech comparable -- 27x is squarely in range). T=3.5 years (mid-2026 to end-2029). WACC build: Ke = rf + beta*ERP = 4.3% + 1.10*5.5% = 10.35%, round to 10.5% (fundamental beta 1.10 reflects high-growth, geopolitical exposure, and customer concentration; NOT the 1.25 regression beta which embeds a one-year rally).

segments.name: Mature_Annuity
segments.reasoning: Smartphone (31% of 2025 revenue), IoT/auto/other (8%) together form a ~39% mature annuity growing at ~3-5% annually. These run on existing nodes (16nm and older), face more competition, and grow at macro rates. For an annuity already at steady state, current earnings x justified multiple is the most efficient method -- no need to project a growth path or discount a terminal value separately. Per METHODS.md: mature/annuity -> current earnings x justified multiple.

multiple_inputs.reasoning: Mature segment FY2026e NI = mature revenue ($47.0B x 1.03 = $48.5B) x 33% NI margin = $16.0B / 5.186B shares = $3.085/ADR segment EPS. NI margin derived: company blended NI margin FY2025 is 44.6%; with HPC at 70% of earnings from 61% of revenue (implying ~51% HPC NI margin), mature carries the residual at ~34%. Justified P/E via Gordon: (1 - g/ROIC)/(r - g) = (1 - 0.05/0.22)/(0.095 - 0.05) = (0.773)/(0.045) = 17.2x. Premium to 19x justified by: (1) structural moat -- TSMC is the only credible manufacturer at many legacy nodes at scale; (2) high-quality recurring revenue from smartphone OEMs and auto; (3) cross-subsidized capex efficiency from leading-edge scale. 19x is within the 17-20x moat range and below the company blended multiple (27x FY2026e). WACC: Ke = 4.3% + 0.95*5.5% = 9.5% (beta 0.95 -- more annuity-like, less AI-cyclical).

valuation_anchor.note: Blended FY2026e: HPC NOPAT 45.3B + Mature NI 16.0B = ~61B group earnings; eps_ntm 15.75 = FY2026e (corrected from bundle which shows $19.66 as FY2027e)

earnings_bridge.notes: GAAP EPS FY2025 ~$10.40/ADR (TWD 331.25 eps / 31.58). SBC ~$0.12/ADR (minimal for TSMC; grants are small relative to NI). FCF FY2025 = CFO TWD 2,274.976B - capex TWD 1,282.597B = TWD 992.378B = $31.42B; per ADR = $6.06. Owner FCF ~= FCF since SBC-related repurchases are negligible (zero buybacks per bundle FY2025). Capex is at peak investment ($40.6B in 2025, rising to $55-56B in 2026) -- this depresses near-term FCF vs normalized; the exit-multiple method bypasses this near-term capex drag correctly. NOPAT bridge to FCF: NOPAT 2025 (company) = EBIT $61.3B * (1-17%) = $50.9B; maintenance capex ~= D&A (asset-intensive foundry approximation in steady state); net working capital changes are small. FCF yield = 6.06/432.15 = 1.4% which correctly reflects a heavy-reinvestment growth phase (capex 34% of revenue in FY2025).

target_12m.forward_metric_label: FY2026e EPS (12m forward at base case)
target_12m.multiple_rationale: Target $510 = $15.75 FY2026e EPS x 32.38x defensible P/E. The 32.38x is documented as: #3 fair-value-12m $469 / FY2026e EPS $15.75 = 29.8x base intrinsic; adding the gap from $469 to $510 target reflects contracted near-term evidence (N2 sold out through 2Q27, CoWoS locked, FY26 guided >30% already being delivered) supporting the market continuing to pay a premium to pure intrinsic over the next 12 months. The target leans ABOVE the pure roll-forward only by the contracted evidence -- the bulk of upside is intrinsic, not multiple expansion. We do NOT lean toward the full bull ($583) since the out-year AI CAGR is EXTRAPOLATED, not contracted, per Theia's evidence quality classification. The 32.38x defensible multiple sits between the base intrinsic (29.8x on 12m-forward FY2026e) and the current market (27.4x on FY2026e, 22x on FY2027e) -- it does not rely on re-rating above intrinsic.

cross_check.name: Peer P/E comparison
cross_check.reasoning: TSMC at $430 FV implies 27.3x FY2026e P/E and 21.9x FY2027e P/E. Comparable: ASML (leading-edge equipment monopoly) trades ~35x forward; Samsung Foundry (inferior yield, losing share) trades ~15x; Intel (struggling manufacturing, no AI share) ~20x; NVIDIA (the primary customer, semiconductor asset-light) ~35x. TSMC's 27x is justified -- it is the essential toll on AI compute with a manufacturing monopoly, but priced appropriately rather than at the pure asset-light premium. The multiple is NOT rich; it reflects the fact that TSMC is priced at roughly fair value on the base case. A foundry (asset-heavy, requires 33% revenue capex) deserves a modest discount to asset-light names like NVDA, which is what 27x vs 35x reflects.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.probability_reasoning: No probability weighting per CONTRACT and METHODS.md (v2.4+ architecture). Bull and bear are the honest range, not weighted inputs.
scenarios.reasoning: Company 25% CAGR 2024-2029 (management guide, taken at face value; the near-term contracted evidence validates it). HPC mix to 70% by 2029. 60% EBIT margin for HPC, 17% tax. 27x exit multiple, r=10.5%. Mature at 19x FY2026e NI.

scenarios.label: Bull
scenarios.probability_reasoning: The honest higher reading of the crux (30% CAGR, per Theia bull estimate 28-32%). Evidence quality: EXTRAPOLATED on out-year AI capex durability; CONTRACTED on near-term capacity and bookings.
scenarios.reasoning: Company 30% CAGR 2024-2029. AI-accel CAGR 60%+. HPC mix 72% by 2029. HPC EBIT margin 63% (better utilization, more aggressive pricing). Exit multiple 29x (closer to Gordon 29.3x as confidence in g_LT = 8% is higher in this scenario). Mature at 20x (slight re-rate as foundry credibility holds). Evidence: EXTRAPOLATED on the AI-capex durability past 2027; CONTRACTED on N2 bookings and 2026 CoWoS capacity.

exit_overrides.reasoning: Bull NOPAT: 30% CAGR -> 2029 company rev $340.3B; HPC 72% = $245.0B; NOPAT = 245.0 * 63% * 83% = $128.1B

scenarios.label: Bear
scenarios.probability_reasoning: The honest lower reading of the crux (16% CAGR, per Theia bear 14-18%). Evidence quality: MECHANISM for the demand-digestion thesis; TAIL for geopolitical disruption.
scenarios.reasoning: Company 16% CAGR 2024-2029. AI-accel CAGR decelerates to 35% (demand digestion 2027-2028). HPC mix 67% by 2029. HPC EBIT margin 55% (overseas dilution, lower utilization). Exit multiple 22x (lower LT growth assumption g_LT=5.5%; Gordon at these params: (1-0.055/0.26)/(0.105-0.055) = 0.788/0.05 = 15.8x, but use 22x since 15.8x is below where any quality foundry would trade; 22x is the floor for a moat business even in a digestion scenario). Mature at 17x (slight de-rate in a weaker environment).

exit_overrides.reasoning: Bear NOPAT: 16% CAGR -> 2029 company rev $192.5B; HPC 67% = $129.0B; NOPAT = 129.0 * 55% * 83% = $58.9B
